title: drosera peltata subsp. auriculata
intro: Drosera peltata subsp. auriculata, also known as the ear-leaved sundew, is a subspecies of carnivorous plant belonging to the Drosera genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, to treat various health issues such as colds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Drosera peltata subsp. auriculata are attributed to its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
